qa_pairs.question: Can a LibreQDA session be reopened if the web browser tab is closed?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is possible to reopen an active LibreQDA session after the web browser tab has been closed.

qa_pairs.question: When does a LibreQDA session terminate?
qa_pairs.answer: The LibreQDA session will end when the JupyterLab session ends.

qa_pairs.question: What is RStudio?
qa_pairs.answer: RStudio is an integrated development environment primarily used for the R language.

qa_pairs.question: How can I use a different version of R software with RStudio?
qa_pairs.answer: You can use a different version of R by loading another version of the `rstudio-server` module. This must be done before launching RStudio.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I launch RStudio before loading a different R version module?
qa_pairs.answer: If you launch RStudio before loading a different R version, you may have to restart your JupyterLab session.

qa_pairs.question: How does the RStudio launcher function?
qa_pairs.answer: The RStudio launcher will open or reopen an RStudio interface in a new web browser tab.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to resume an active RStudio session after closing the browser tab?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is possible to reopen an active RStudio session after the web browser tab was closed.

qa_pairs.question: When does an RStudio session conclude?
qa_pairs.answer: The RStudio session will end when the JupyterLab session ends.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended way to end an RStudio session and release resources?
qa_pairs.answer: To end your session and release resources, you should use the `File > Log Out` menu item on the JupyterLab browser tab.

qa_pairs.question: Does quitting RStudio or closing browser tabs release its resources?
qa_pairs.answer: No, simply quitting RStudio or closing the RStudio and JupyterHub tabs in your browser will not release the resources (CPU, memory, GPU) or end the underlying Slurm job.

qa_pairs.question: What is MLflow?
qa_pairs.answer: MLflow is an open-source platform designed to help machine learning practitioners and teams manage the complexities of the machine learning process, focusing on ensuring each phase is manageable, traceable, and reproducible.

qa_pairs.question: Can I use a different version of MLflow than the default?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you may use a different version of MLflow by loading a specific `mlflow` module.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more information about using MLflow for AI experiments?
qa_pairs.answer: More information on how to use MLflow to track your AI experiments can be found on the [[MLflow]] page.

qa_pairs.question: What is OpenRefine used for?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenRefine is a powerful, free, and open-source tool for cleaning up messy data, transforming it, and extending it to add value. It is commonly used to correct typos in manually collected survey data.

qa_pairs.question: How long does it take for a new OpenRefine session to start?
qa_pairs.answer: For a new session, the OpenRefine session can take up to 3 minutes to complete its startup.

qa_pairs.question: Can an OpenRefine session be resumed after closing the browser tab?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is possible to reopen an active OpenRefine session after the web browser tab was closed.

qa_pairs.question: When does an OpenRefine session terminate?
qa_pairs.answer: The OpenRefine session will end when the JupyterLab session ends.

qa_pairs.question: What does Tensorboard provide?
qa_pairs.answer: Tensorboard provides the visualization and tooling needed for machine learning experimentation. It enables tracking experiment metrics like loss and accuracy, visualizing the model graph, and projecting embeddings to a lower dimensional space.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to use a different version of Tensorboard?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you can change the version of Tensorboard if a different module is available.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more details on using the Tensorboard software package?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find more details on using this software package on the [[Tensorboard]] page.

qa_pairs.question: What desktop environments are available by default in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: Two different Desktop environments are available by default: Mate Desktop and XFCE Desktop.

qa_pairs.question: Which default desktop environment offers a more modern user interface?
qa_pairs.answer: XFCE Desktop yields a more modern UI.

qa_pairs.question: Which default desktop environment is lighter to use?
qa_pairs.answer: Mate Desktop is lighter to use.

qa_pairs.question: How do the desktop launchers in JupyterLab function?
qa_pairs.answer: These launchers will open or reopen a remote Linux desktop interface in a new web browser tab.

qa_pairs.question: What is the advantage of using JupyterLab for desktop sessions compared to VNC and SSH?
qa_pairs.answer: Using JupyterLab for desktop sessions eliminates the need to run a VNC server on a compute node, create an SSH tunnel, or use a VNC client.

qa_pairs.question: How long does it take for a new Desktop session to start?
qa_pairs.answer: For a new session, the Desktop session can take up to 3 minutes to complete its startup.

qa_pairs.question: Can an active desktop session be reopened after closing the web browser tab?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is possible to reopen an active desktop session after the web browser tab was closed.

qa_pairs.question: When does a desktop session end in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: The desktop session will end when the JupyterLab session ends.
